# digg-pp-cli build log

## What was built

### Generated by the press (Phase 2)
- `feed raw` — GET /ai (HTML scrape)
- `feed story-raw` — GET /ai/{clusterUrlId}
- `trending` (promoted from `trending status`) — GET /api/trending/status
- Built-in: `agent-context`, `api`, `completion`, `doctor`, `feedback`, `import`, `profile`, `which`, `workflow`, `version`

### Hand-built in Phase 3
- `internal/diggparse/` — RSC stream decoder + cluster/event extractor (160 LOC + 100 LOC tests)
- `internal/diggstore/` — schema migrations and persistence (5 tables + 1 FTS5 + indexes)
- `internal/cli/digg_sync.go` — replaces generated sync; fetches /ai, parses RSC, persists clusters/authors/events; runs replacement archaeology
- `internal/cli/digg_commands.go` — 10 novel commands + 3 supporting (open, stats):
  - `top` — top clusters from local store
  - `rising` — sort by delta
  - `story <id>` — full detail
  - `search <query>` — FTS5 over title/label/tldr/source
  - `events --since --type` — pipeline event stream
  - `evidence <id>` — scoreComponents + numeratorLabel + percentAboveAverage
  - `sentiment <id> --window 6h|12h|24h` — positivity ratios
  - `crossref <id>` — HN + Techmeme + external references
  - `replaced --since` — knocked-out clusters with rationale
  - `history <id>` — rank trajectory from snapshots
  - `authors top --by influence|posts|reach` — Digg AI 1000 leaderboard
  - `author <username> --since` — every cluster a given X account contributed to
  - `watch --interval --min-delta --alert` — read-only rank-movement watcher
  - `pipeline status [--watch]` — operational dashboard
  - `open <id> [--launch]` — print-by-default safe URL launcher
  - `stats` — local store metrics

## Spec

`internal/research/digg-spec.yaml` — 75 lines. Three resources:
- `trending.status` (real JSON API)
- `feed.raw` (HTML scrape; CLI parses RSC)
- `feed.story_raw` (HTML scrape per cluster URL ID)

The bulk of the CLI surface (10 novel commands) is hand-built in Phase 3 because the data path is HTML-RSC scrape + local SQLite, which the generator's REST-API endpoint pattern cannot express.

## Discovery findings that shaped the build
- The /ai page server ignores `?filter=` query strings and always returns the same `top` snapshot. All filter modes (top, rising, fastest, engaged, positive, climbing) are computed client-side from one fetch. CLI does the same locally.
- /api/trending/status is the single real public JSON API and exposes a live event stream of pipeline activity. This is the killer feature — no other surface gives this.
- robots.txt disallows /api/ but /api/trending/status is intentionally exposed (returns application/json from a real handler, not the SPA shell). The CLI uses it.
- Only one topic `/ai` exists; sitemap lists `/ai/influencers` but it currently 404s.
- Auth: Clerk (di.gg) but the CLI never authenticates — all reads are public.

## Intentionally deferred
- No body fields skipped — the spec only has GETs.
- `--with-details` flag on sync exists but is opt-in; would fetch every cluster's detail page which is high-cost.
- The `--alert` flag on `watch` accepts only `rank.delta>=N` for now; richer expression language deferred.
- No write/mutation features. Read-only ethical scope per the absorb gate.

## Generator limitations encountered
- The generated `sync.go` walked spec resources expecting REST endpoints; for an HTML-scrape primary surface it produced 0 records. Workaround: hand-built `digg_sync.go` replaces the generated sync via `registerDiggCommands`.
- The internal YAML spec format does not have a clean way to express "HTML page → embedded RSC stream → cluster array". Expressing it as a single GET-returning-RawHTML resource works for generation but the parsing must be hand-built.
- `printOutput` and `truncate` are exported helpers in the generator's emitted `internal/cli/helpers.go`; my code initially redeclared them. Rename to `diggTruncate` and `printClusterOutput` resolved cleanly.
